Developer's Certificate of Origin for JerryScript contributions, requiring signed-off-by process.

# Purpose
The JerryScript Developer's Certificate of Origin (DCO) outlines the process and requirements for contributing code to the JerryScript project. Contributors must include a signed-off-by tag in the commit message of every patch, which serves as a certification that they have the right to submit the contribution under the specified open source license. The certification confirms that the contribution is original, based on appropriately licensed previous work, or provided by another certified individual. The DCO ensures a clear chain of trust and maintains a public record of contributions, including personal information, under the same open source license. The format for the signed-off-by tag is specified, and contributions without this certification cannot be merged into the mainline.
